sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: I can see the white dot appeared inside the red diamond. So SPACE cycled from orange X to red diamond. Now I need to move the red diamond to collect the 3 red targets at (3, 21), (9, 27), and (12, 12).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
white = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W']
red_dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'r' and n['pixels'] == 1]

print("White dot:", [n['boundary'] for n in white])
print("Red dots:", len(red_dots))

# Diamond center is at white dot position
center = white[0]['boundary'][0]
print(f"Red diamond center: {center}")

# Target red dots (not on diamond edges from center)
# Diamond edges from center (r, c) with radius 9:
# TL: tr + tc = r + c - 9
# TR: -tr + tc = c - r + 9
# BR: tr + tc = r + c + 9
# BL: -tr + tc = c - r - 9

r, c = center
targets = []
for n in red_dots:
    tr, tc = n['boundary'][0][0], n['boundary'][0][1]
    if tr == r and tc == c:
        continue  # skip center
    on_edge = False
    if tr + tc == r + c - 9 and abs(tr - r) <= 9:
        on_edge = True
    if -tr + tc == c - r + 9 and abs(tr - r) <= 9:
        on_edge = True
    if tr + tc == r + c + 9 and abs(tr - r) <= 9:
        on_edge = True
    if -tr + tc == c - r - 9 and abs(tr - r) <= 9:
        on_edge = True
    if not on_edge:
        targets.append((tr, tc))

print(f"Target red dots: {targets}")

# Find optimal center to collect all targets
# For each target, compute which edge equations it satisfies
for t in targets:
    tr, tc = t
    print(f"Target ({tr},{tc}): TL→r+c={tr+tc+9}, TR→c-r={tc-tr-9}, BR→r+c={tr+tc-9}, BL→c-r={tc-tr+9}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Red dots: 39

Target (3,21): TL→r+c=33, TR→c-r=9, BR→r+c=15, BL→c-r=27
Target (9,27): TL→r+c=45, TR→c-r=9, BR→r+c=27, BL→c-r=27
Target (12,12): TL→r+c=33, TR→c-r=-9, BR→r+c=15, BL→c-r=9

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good. The red diamond center is at (30, 39). The 3 targets are (3, 21), (9, 27), and (12, 12).
Looking at the edge equations:
- (3, 21) on TR: c - r = 9
- (9, 27) on TR: c - r = 9
- (12, 12) on BL: c - r = 9
All three can be collected when c - r = 9. Now I need to find the right r value.
With c = r + 9:
- (3, 21) on TR: |3 - r| ≤ 9 → r ∈ [-6, 12]
- (9, 27) on TR: |9 - r| ≤ 9 → r ∈ [0, 18]
- (12, 12) on BL: |12 - r| ≤ 9 → r ∈ [3, 21]
Combined: r ∈ [3, 12]
Let me pick r = 9, c = 18. From current (30, 39):
- Row: 30 → 9, need 21/3 = 7 UP
- Col: 39 → 18, need 21/3 = 7 LEFT
Total: 14 moves.
But wait, I should double-check: when the diamond moves, the targets need to be within the arm length of 9 from the center.
For (3, 21) on TR edge from (9, 18): k = 3 - 9 = -6, tc = 18 + 9 + (-6) = 21 ✓, |k| = 6 ≤ 9 ✓
For (9, 27) on TR edge from (9, 18): k = 9 - 9 = 0, tc = 18 + 9 + 0 = 27 ✓, |k| = 0 ≤ 9 ✓
For (12, 12) on BL edge from (9, 18): k = 12 - 9 = 3, tc = 18 - 9 - 3 = 6 ≠ 12. Wait, that's wrong.
Let me redo the edge equations. The diamond has vertices at (r-9, c), (r, c+9), (r+9, c), (r, c-9).
Top-right edge: from (r-9, c) to (r, c+9). Parametric: (r-9+k, c+k) for k from 0 to 9.
So tr = r - 9 + k, tc = c + k. Eliminating k: tc - tr = c - r + 9. ✓
For (9, 27): 27 - 9 = 18. c - r + 9 = 18 → c - r = 9. ✓
k = tc - c = 27 - 18 = 9. And tr = r - 9 + k = 9 - 9 + 9 = 9. ✓
Bottom-left edge: from (r+9, c) to (r, c-9). Parametric: (r+9-k, c-k) for k from 0 to 9.
So tr = r + 9 - k, tc = c - k. Eliminating k: tc - tr = c - r - 9.
For (12, 12): 12 - 12 = 0. c - r - 9 = 0 → c - r = 9. ✓
k = c - tc = 18 - 12 = 6. And tr = r + 9 - k = 9 + 9 - 6 = 12. ✓
Top-right edge for (3, 21):
k = tc - c = 21 - 18 = 3. And tr = r - 9 + k = 9 - 9 + 3 = 3. ✓
All three targets are on the diamond edges when center is at (9, 18). Now let me move there.
From (30, 39) to (9, 18): 7 UP, 7 LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move red diamond from (30, 39) to (9, 18) to collect all 3 red targets
# 7 UP, 7 LEFT = 14 moves
action(['UP']*7 + ['LEFT']*7)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
